title: Alsine octandra
intro: Alsine octandra, also known as Alsine or Mountain Avens, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as potential anti-diabetic and anti-cancer activities. The bioactive constituents of Alsine octandra include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Alsine octandra is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs in tinctures or capsules to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Alsine octandra is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It belongs to the genus Alsine, which includes various species of grasses and flowering plants.
